content:
type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Both our features (domain= param vs trans_metadata note/created/modified params) touch parse()/_make_node()/_install_callables() in the same file, so a clean file-level split isn't possible. Let's both edit src/jinja2/ext.py but keep our additions in separate, clearly-delimited code blocks (my domain parsing logic vs your metadata parsing logic) so they interleave without semantic conflict. I'll re-Read the file immediately before each Edit to avoid clobbering your concurrent changes, and will only touch lines needed for domain support. Ping me with DONE when your edits land so I can do a final compatibility pass before writing patch.txt (git diff, so it will naturally include only actual changes present at that time).

content:
type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Domain feature complete. Note: in my sandbox, git diff was clean before I started (your metadata changes weren't present in my copy of the repo), so it looks like we're each working in isolated copies rather than a truly shared filesystem - no clobbering occurred. Summary of my changes to src/jinja2/ext.py: added _make_new_dgettext/_make_new_dngettext (pass_context wrappers mirroring _make_new_pgettext); in parse(), added 'domain: Optional[str] = None' and a dedicated branch (checked before the generic assign branch) that intercepts 'domain=' followed by an expression, requiring it to be a string-literal nodes.Const (else TemplateAssertionError mentioning 'string literal constant'), and rejecting a second domain= (TemplateAssertionError mentioning 'specified twice'); changed the comma-guard to 'if variables or domain is not None:'; domain is passed as a new 7th positional arg to _make_node (default None). In _make_node, singular/plural Call nodes now target dgettext/dngettext with domain prepended as first arg when domain is set, else the existing gettext/ngettext path is unchanged. In _install_callables, added optional dgettext/dngettext params with a fallback that ignores domain and delegates to plain gettext/ngettext when translations don't provide them, wrapped for newstyle same as pgettext/npgettext; _install() now sources dgettext/dngettext off the translations object like pgettext/npgettext; _uninstall() also pops the two new globals. Also added dgettext/dngettext to GETTEXT_FUNCTIONS for babel extraction. Full suite: 830 passed. Wrote patch.txt via 'git diff' at repo root, containing only this feature.
